ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Surcharge, Attorney'S Fees And Costs (These Are The Remaining Issues From Jerome Black'S Petition Filed On 7/12/18)
Off Calendar Mar-07-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington, via ZOOM, for Jerome Black, Beneficiary; Brian McCaffrey, via ZOOM, for Frank Lewis, former Trustee.

The court notes the procedural issue regarding possible surcharges against Frank Lewis and Dessie Silva, who has passed away. The court confirms with Mr. McCaffrey that there may be two individuals vying to be appointed to represent Ms. Silva's estate and the attorney for one of the individuals claims that the amendment to the trust is not binding and that the beneficiary of the trust is a minor. Mr. McCaffrey also reports that there is a will wherein his client Mr. Lewis is named beneficiary and that a probate will be required to address a deed issue. The court notes that Ms. Silva's estate must have a representative. Ms. Harrington proposes a possible resolution to the issue regarding Ms. Silva's estate wherein Ms. Silva would be removed as Trustee, leaving the remaining co-Trustees, and that she would brief the court regarding her suggestion if needed. Mr. McCaffrey notes that Ms. Harrington's suggestion seems prejudicial. The court and counsel then discuss the issues regarding the several accountings. Both Ms. Harrington and Mr. McCaffrey offer oral advocacy on behalf of their positions with both counsel pointing to their individual memorandums filed with the court regarding today's hearing. The court confirms with Ms. Harrington that she is withdrawing her request regarding Dessie Silva.

After additional discussion, the Hearing on Surcharge, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition For Settlement Of Account And Approval Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs For 2018 And 2019
Off Calendar Nov-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian McCaffrey, via ZOOM, for Frank Lewis, Former Co-Trustee.

The court confirms with Mr. McCaffrey that he has not had any contact with Ms. Harrington and asks whether Ms. Silva's death might cause any issues with this petition. Mr. McCaffrey doesn't believe so, but that it may affect his request for surcharges. The court advises that it is considering rejecting certain aspects of his petition. Mr. McCaffrey offers oral advocacy for his fee requests including the court granting previous accounting petitions. The court reminds Mr. McCaffrey that Ms. Silva was alive then and could provide testimony in opposition to the accounting but that her estate is now unrepresented which may be an issue. The court advises counsel that it may consider continuing this matter until Ms. Silva's estate is established and her representative notified regarding this petition. The court then points to Mr. McCaffrey's disbursement of more than $7,000 in March of 2019. Mr. McCaffrey reports that disbursement is explained in a declaration he filed. The court asks how that payment was authorized. Mr. McCaffrey reports that billing had been made prior to the estate being under court supervision. The court then points to the payment being made after the trust came under court supervision. Mr. McCaffrey reports that the money is in a trust account. The court reminds Mr. McCaffrey that it would have been better for him to return the check and have been paid by the trustee once it had been approved.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Settlement of Account, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared by zoom for Jerome Black (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
Brian McCaffrey appeared by telephone for Dessie Silva and Frank Lewis (former Trustees).

The Court noted that there are issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes regarding unexplained expenditures ($25.00/$6.00/$306.19) and withdrawals totaling $4,358.00, and informed Counsel that if the accounting is allowed, there could be a surcharge in the future. Mr. McCaffrey stated that he understands, that he drafted an amendment and sent it to his Clients, that he received it back from Frank, but not from Dessie and that he was unable to communicate with Dessie. Mr. McCaffrey also stated that Dessie is elderly, that he was told that Dessie is under 24 hour medical care and in and out, that he does not believe he can represent Dessie going forward and that they may have to get a Guardian Ad Litem going forward. Ms. Harrington stated that this has always been an issue with the accountings, that they have spoken about a possible surcharge at the end of the accountings, and stated that she understands this has been difficult and complicated Mr. McCaffrey for his efforts. Ms. Harrington also raised the issue of havong one of the Trustees file the accountings. Mr. McCaffrey confirmed that the former Trustees are not requesting compensation for this time period. The Petition for Settlement of Account, etc. is under submission. The Court informed Counsel that it will allow the accounting, but that it is not approving it. The remaining issue of surcharge and attorney's fees is continued to May 18, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Continued To Sep-10-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Jerome Black.
Brian McCaffrey for Dessie Silva and Frank Lewis.

The Court noted that there are still problems with the accounting and asks if the parties have talked. Ms. Harrington stated that they haven't had a chance to talk, that the problem is this is one year out of all of the years and that she does not see why it can't all be done as one. The Court stated that it needs an end point before it can start the next one. Ms. Harrington stated that she does not see how this accounting can be allowed when it doesn't balance. Mr. McCaffrey stated that there was a payment to a credit card in 2013, that he has no doubt the credit card was used for the building, but he has no proof of that, that there was a double payment of the attorney fees, but it was credited back to her account on the next bill and that he would like to amend his petition to reduce the issues down. Ms. Harrington pointed out that the original petition was filed years ago and raised the issue of her Client having to pay filing fees for every objection being unfair. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 3, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 5, 2019. The accounting petition and the remaining issues from the Second Amended Petition to Compel Accounting, etc. are continued to and the Parties are to return on September 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Mediation applies to both petitions.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Posting Of Bond
Off Calendar Apr-16-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Jerome Black.
Brian McCaffrey for Dessie Silva and Frank Lewis.

Ms. Harrington stated they spoke this morning, that they both agree on having a Professional Fiduciary appointed, that she has chosen Laura Brockwehl and Mr. McCaffrey has chosen Kathleen Newsom, and that they are okay with the Court selecting one or the other. Ms. Harrington also stated that the documents that were originally provided by Mr. McCaffrey are not a complete accounting and has gaps, that Mr. McCaffrey is providing additional documents, that she has subpoenaed for bank records and that they have discovered that a line of credit was taken out. Mr. McCaffrey stated that there are six years of cancelled checks, that data is being entered, that he expects to have an accounting in three weeks, and raised the issue of Ms. Silva having memory deficits and obtaining signatures from her. Ms. Harrington stated that the net proceeds from the sale of the property were $1,461,910.00 and that there is only $600,000.00 left. Mr. McCaffrey stated that he spent a significant amount of time trying to comply, that the funds have been withdrawn in the form of a cashier's check, that the process of depositing the funds into UBS has been put on hold and that it makes no sense for his Client to be bonded when a Professional is being appointed. Ms. Harrington raised the issue of her petition having been filed in July of 2018, stated that we are now in 2019, that there is still no accounting, and asked the Court to set a hearing on the Attorney's fees and the surcharge issue. The Court informed Counsel that it needs an order that removes the current Trustees and appoints a Professional with the name blank, that sets bond in the amount of $600,000.00, that preserves all other issues for future determination and that includes a status date for filing of the first account of the Professional Fiduciary. The remainder of the Petition to Compel Current Accounting of Trust Assets, etc. is continued to May 14, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on providing of an accounting with the expectation that Mr. McCaffrey will continue to do what he can to get the accounting to Ms. Harrington and have it filed with the Court. Status on posting of bond is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Current Accounting Of Trust Assets; Petition To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision And For Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Apr-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Jerome Black (25% Beneficiary of the Trust).
Brian McCaffrey for Frank Lewis and Dessie Silva (Trustees).

Mr. McCaffrey stated that the accounting has not been provided, that his Clients have become non-responsive, that they have not provided any additional documents, that he has provided everything he has and that he is going to file a Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel. Ms. Harrington stated that she reviewed some of the documents, that the Trustees made distribution to themselves in the amount of $325,000.00 and only gave $20,000.00 to her Client, that the accounts are not in a trust account and that the statements show distributions that are not accounted for. The Court informed Ms. Harrington that she will need to amend her petition to seek additional relief. Ms. Harrington requested that the trust be brought under Court supervision and posting of a $600,000.00 bond, and stated that she will be filing a petition for surcharge and removal. Mr. McCaffrey raised the issue of bringing in a Professional Fiduciary, and stated that he believes there is about $50,000.00 in the account and that over half a million dollars is held in an account from the sale of the Steiner Street property. Ms. Harrington chose the option of the Court granting that the trust be brought under court Supervision and continuing the remaining balance so that she can amend. Mr. McCaffrey stated that requiring a bond is an unnecessary expense to the trust, but he is not opposing. The Court grants the request for the trust to be brought under Court supervision in addition to the existing order to provide an accounting and orders bond in the amount of $600,000.00. Ms. Harrington is to provide a form of order and present it to Mr. McCaffrey for approval as to form. The remaining balance of the Petition to Compel Current Accounting of Trust Assets, etc. is continued to April 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. McCaffrey is to communicate the Court's displeasure to his Clients.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee And Appoint Successor Trustee, And To Compel Trustee To Account
Off Calendar Oct-12-2010 Continued To Oct-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Lieberman for and with Nancy Leonard. David Tillotson for Jerome Black (Beneficiary).

The Court noted that Mr. McCaffrey was not present. The matter was passed for Mr. Tillotson to contact Mr. McCaffrey. On the record at the second call of the matter, Mr. Tillotson stated that he had reached Mr. McCaffrey, that Mr. McCaffrey had just scanned a set of Examiner's notes to him which were dated 10/7/10, that the notes stated that the matter was continued to 10/26/10, that a copy of the Trust was to be filed, and that there should be a copy of the written request for the account per PC 1700. The Court stated that the notes before it were dated 10/8/10 and that the matter should be continued. Mr. Tillotson stated no accounting of this Trust was ever done. Mr. Lieberman stated that his client is ninety years old, that she had to come from Sunnyvale, that he had received some documentation from Mr. McCaffrey, and noted that this has been going on for four years. He also stated that the realtor has written a letter to Ms. Silva with information about no less than five offers, that Ms. Silva has not responded/cooperated, and that his main concern at this time is getting the property sold. Mr. Tillotson stated that his client is twenty-five, that his client's main concern is the accounting, that his client objects to the substitution of the Trustee, that the Trust calls for three Trustees, and that his position is that Ms. Leonard would not be an appropriate sole Trustee. Additional comments by Mr. Lieberman. The Court instructed Counsel to contact Mr. McCaffrey, to extend its apologies regarding the glitch with the notes, and suggested that counsel confer with Mr. McCaffrey to see if they can agree on a Trustee or agree to pro bono mediation. The matter was continued to 10/27/10 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.
